title: AUC-First Race-Grouped Ranker
summary: ROC AUC rewards ordering, not calibrated probabilities.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ordering, not calibrated probabilities. Ranking losses such as CatBoost YetiRankPairwise or LightGBM LambdaRank can directly learn to rank imminent pit stops above same-race negatives, adding diversity to logloss-trained classifiers.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoostRanker/LightGBM ranker with query groups such as Race_Year or Race_Year+Stint. Use existing engineered features, rank-normalize predictions within Race_Year, then blend raw rank scores with the best classifier probabilities.
expected_effect: Improves pairwise ordering around rare pit events and may lift AUC even if probabilities are less calibrated.
risk: Too-narrow query groups can hurt global ordering across races; validate several grouping schemes.
